zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabet de la Warr於大約1327年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾韋斯頓 法維爾時，她的父親John de la Warre34歲，母親Margaret de Holand20歲。她和John Chiseldon至少有1個兒子。她去世在英國戴文霍爾可姆 洛古斯，埋葬在英國戴文霍爾可姆 洛古斯哥吉拉 沙也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Warr
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname for a warrior, usually from Norman French were, werre, waire, warre (central French guerre) ‘war, battle’, and short for de la werre (de la guerre, see Delaware ), literally ‘(man) of the war’, a construction paralleled in de la Bataille (see Battle ). Occasionally it is a shortened form of Old French werreur ‘warrior’.
English: variant of   Ware  .

variants.name: Warrior
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Jogesh, Krishna, Rahul.
English (North Yorkshire): of Norman origin, an occupational name or nickname from Old Norman French werreieor, werrieur ‘warrior, soldier’. Compare Warr .
English: in northern England alternatively a variant of Quarrier, an occupational name from Middle English quarriour ‘quarryman’, Old French quarreour, quarrier.

variants.name: Ware
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by or looked after a dam or weir on a river (from Old English wær, wer, Middle English war(e), warre, wer(e), werre ‘weir’), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Weare (Somerset), Weare Giffard (Devon), or Ware (Hertfordshire).
topographic name for someone who lived by a horse pond or fish pond, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English wayour, which became early modern English ware, wear.

variants.name: Wickware
meanings: English: habitational name from Wickwar in Gloucestershire (Avon), originally called simply Wick, from Old English wīc ‘outlying settlement’. The war element is from the name of the de la Warr family (see Warr ), who held the manor in the 13th century.
History: John Wickware moved from England to New London, CT, in 1675.

variants.name: Delaware
meanings: English:
(of Norman origin): variant of Warr 1, with fused Old French preposition de and definite article la ‘from the’.

possibly also a habitational name from a farm called Delaware in Brasted (Kent), said to be named with Old French de la ‘from the’ + Middle English ware ‘weir’ though this seems unlikely. The surname Delaware is virtually non-existing in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is my oath’. See Elisabeth .

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabet de la Warr was born about 1327, in Weston Favell, Northamptonshire, England, her father, John de la Warre, was 34 and her mother, Margaret de Holand, was 20. She had at least 1 son with John Chiseldon. She died in Holcombe Rogus, Devon, England, and was buried in All Saints' Church, Holcombe Rogus, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabet de la Warrが約1327年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアウエストン ファベルで生まれた時，父John de la Warreは34歳，母Margaret de Holandは20歳であった。彼女はJohn Chiseldonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女はイギリスデボンホールコンベ ログスで死去し，イギリスデボンホールコンベ ログスアル サイントス' チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.All Saints' Church, Holcombe Rogus, Devo: 영국 데폰 홀코브 로구스 앨 세인츠' 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabet de la Warr(이)가 대략 1327년 영국 노르섬프토실르 웨스턴 파벨에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de la Warre(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Holand(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 John Chiseldon(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 데폰 홀코브 로구스에서 사망했으며 영국 데폰 홀코브 로구스 앨 세인츠' 처르치에 묻혔다.

ru.All Saints' Church, Holcombe Rogus, Devo: алл сеинц' чурч, холкомб рогус, девон, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabet de la Warr родилась около 1327 г. в местности вестон фавелл, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de la Warre было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Holand, 20. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от John Chiseldon. Она умерла в местности холкомб рогус, девон, Англия и была похоронена в местности алл сеинц' чурч, холкомб рогус, девон, Англия.
